name: scouts-blind
summary: A concealed observation post — a hide of branches, cloth, and camouflage overlooking a trail or landmark. Room for one or two watchers, barely.

primary_palette.note: woven branch blind, bedroll on the ground, kit stashed low
prone watcher position — no shelter, just camouflage overhead
seated blind with a spyglass mount pointed outward

story_prompts: occupied — a scout is watching the trail below, unaware of the party behind
abandoned mid-watch — still-warm coffee, notes on parchment, no scout in sight
the scout is a spy for the enemy — the notes incriminate someone back home
days-old blind — weathered, but the notes hint at a pattern the party needs
two blinds, crossfire position — the watchers have been covering a single approach

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: outdoor-dim — deliberately shaded, no fire (would give position away)

anti_patterns: no fire — the whole point is to not be seen
keep it tiny — a blind that fits four people is a camp, not a blind
no built walls — brush and bush-large define the concealment
one clear sightline out — the rest of the perimeter is densely screened
